sourceFile: Biology (Problem of the Day) - one-eighth to finals.docx
subject: Biology
questionText: Some athletes tend to train at higher altitudes for a period of time before major competitions. Give a physiological explanation for the benefit gained by athletes who train at high altitudes before competitions.
correctAnswer: The oxygen level at high altitude is very low. This will stimulate an increased production of red blood cells and haemoglobin. If an athlete trains at higher altitude for long enough for this to take effect, he/she will have the ability to carry a higher amount of oxygen in his/her blood stream. This effect will continue even at lower altitudes for some weeks or months after the high altitude training has stopped. High oxygen content of the blood results in more energy through aerobic cellular respiration for muscular contraction

sourceFile: Biology (Problem of the Day) - one-eighth to finals.docx
subject: Biology
questionText: Describe how energy ﬂows from the sun through the various trophic levels in a forest ecosystem. Give reasons for the loss of energy from one trophic level to another in a food chain
correctAnswer: Energy from the sun is trapped by green plants in the forest during photosynthesis, producing chemical energy food or carbohydrates Green plants are producers and occupy the first trophic level Green plants are eaten by herbivores (primary producers) as they occupy the second trophic level Herbivores are eaten by carnivores (secondary consumers), which occupy the third trophic level When organisms (plant and animals) die, fungi and bacteria (decomposers and detrivores) which are saprophytic organisms feed on them thus causing them to decompose into simple substances. Decomposers feed on dead organic matter hence cause decomposition and decay which release nutrients for plants, linking biotic and abiotic components i. Inefficient utilization of food resources (wastage) Through respiration Through excretion e.g. urination and sweating

subject: Biology
questionText: In a genetics class, the students were made to cross true-breeding tall and hairy stemmed okro plants with true breeding dwarf and hairless okro plants. They found that all the F1 generation produced as the result of the cross were tall and hairy stemmed. The F1 plants were crossed with each other to obtain 1200 F2 plants. Of these 1200 F2 okro plants, they observed the following numbers of four different phenotypes, Phonotype Number of plants Tall and hairy plants 678 Tall and hairless stem-stemmed plants 230 Dwarf and hairy-stemmed 220 Dwarf and hairless-stemmed plants 72 Which height characteristic is dominant, tallness or dwarfness? Which stem characteristic is dominant, hairiness or hairless. Using the alleles, T for tallness, t for dwarfness, H for hairiness, and h for hairless, state the genotypes of the original true breeding plants. Using the same alleles as in c above, state the genotypes of the F1 hybrid. Using the same alleles, state the genotypes of the four types of okro plants found in the F2 generation? What were the expected numbers okro plants of each type in the F2 generation? Why do the observed number of okro plants of each type in the F2 generation differ from the expected?
correctAnswer: Tallness is dominant Hairiness is dominant The tall hairy-stemmed true-breeding parent has a genotype TTHH. The dwarf hairless-stemmed true breeding parent has the genotype tthh The F1 plants have the same genotype, TtHh Tall and hairy-stemmed okro plants : TTHH, TTHh, TtHh, TtHH Tall and hairless stemmed okro plants: TThh, Tthh Dwarf and hairy stemmed okro plants: ttHH, ttHh Dwarf and hairless stemmed okro plants : tthh Since it follows the 9:3:3:1, pattern the expected numbers were as follows Tall and hairy-stemmed okro plants: 675 Tall and hairless stemmed okro plants: 225 Dwarf and hairy stemmed okro plants: 225 Dwarf and hairless stemmed okro plants: 75 The expected numbers are based on probabilities. The actual numbers should be close to the expected, but would not be exactly the same as the expected numbers

questionText: DNA replication is semiconservative, meaning that each strand in the DNA double helix acts as a template for the synthesis of a new, complementary strand. This process takes us from one starting molecule to two "daughter" molecules, with each newly formed double helix containing one new and one old strand. Each new DNA molecule contains one strand from a parent template and a newly synthesized strand Using the letters O to represent original strands of a parent DNA, and N1, N2 and N3 to represent new complementary strands after 1st, 2nd and 3rd replications respectively, determine the strands of the DNA after three successive replications. Using the letters provided, state and list DNA molecules that do not contain an original strand at the end of the second replication. Determine the number of DNA molecules that contain a parent strand at the end of the third replication. How many of the DNA molecules produced at the end of the third replication do not contain any of the strands produced at the end of the first replication?
correctAnswer: 1. O = original strand Before replication PARENT OO FIRST REPLICATION ON1 ON1 SECOND REPLICATION ON2 N1N2 ON2 N1N2 THIRD REPLICATION ON3 N2N3 N1N3 N2N3 ON3 N2N3 N1N3 N2N3 2. Two of them (all N1N2) 3. Two of them (all ON3) 4. 4 of them and they are all N2N3

subject: Biology
questionText: People with diabetes mellitus normally take their insulin as an injection. Using your knowledge in what happens in the stomach and small intestines of humans in terms of digestion and absorption, explain why insulin is not taken as a pill. An Individual who has had his gall bladder removed was advised to reduce the intake of fat containing foods. What effect will the removal of the individual’s gall bladder have on his ability to absorb and digest fat?
correctAnswer: People with diabetes mellitus normally take their insulin as an injection. Using your knowledge in what happens in the stomach and small intestines of humans in terms of digestion and absorption, explain why insulin is not taken as a pill. Insulin is a protein Protein digestion begins in the stomach The pepsin produced by the glands in the stomach will break down the insulin which is a protein The insulin will therefore not make its way to the small intestine where most substances are absorbed. An Individual who has had his gall bladder removed was advised to reduce the intake of fat containing foods. What effect will the removal of the individual’s gall bladder have on his ability to absorb and digest fat? The gallbladder is a small storage organ located inferior and posterior to the liver. The gallbladder holds bile produced in the liver until it is needed for digesting fatty foods in the duodenum of the small intestine. Without normal amounts and concentrations of bile, the individual will have difficulty digesting and absorbing fat. Fats will remain in the intestines and cause abdominal discomfort. The Individual may also suffer from fat soluble vitamin deficiency due to the difficulty the individual will have in absorbing fat

subject: Chemistry
questionText: In a form three class at a senior high school, the students were are asked to design an experiment to see whether vitamin A improves night vision. They selected 100 general arts students and gave 50 of the students extra vitamin A for 6 weeks and then compared their night vision with that of the rest of the class. As a member of the science class explain the following (a) How you would select the group to be given the extra vitamin A (b) How would you standardize their intake of vitamins? (c) What tests would you conduct before giving the vitamin supplement? (d) How would you record and test the students’ night vision? (e) How would you analyze your results?
correctAnswer: Initially, any student already taking vitamin A supplements would be excluded as well as students on a special diet. The group to receive the supplement would be selected at random, and the experimenter should not know which students were in which group. Students would be asked not to change their diet from their normal one during the course of the experiment. Vitamin A tablets of a recommended dose would then be taken at intervals specified by a dietician or doctor. Students' night vision should be tested at the start of the experiment to establish a 'base line' for each student. A standard test card could be used, at a specified distance, in a dimly lit room not influenced by changes in the outside light intensity. Left and right eyes would be tested separately. A method of scoring would have to be devised, e.g. the number of correct identifications, weighted for the size of type. A standard time for students to become acclimatized to the dim light before starting the test would have to be decided. The mean score for each group would be calculated and the difference determined. It would then have to be judged whether this difference was significantly greater than the differences within either of the groups

subject: Biology
questionText: Give me three differences between primary and secondary succession.
correctAnswer: Primary succession starts on an uninhabited or virgin ground while secondary succession starts on a previously colonized area. Primary succession starts with lower organisms (e.g. lichens) while secondary succession starts with relatively complex organisms. It takes a longer time for primary succession to reach a climax phase while it takes a relatively shorter period for secondary succession to do so

subject: Biology
questionText: Give me three differences between skeletal and smooth muscles.
correctAnswer: Skeletal muscles have cross striations while smooth muscles do not. Skeletal muscles fatigue faster while smooth muscles fatigue relatively slowly. Activities of skeletal muscle are controlled voluntarily while those of smooth muscles are controlled by the autonomic (involuntary) nervous system. Skeletal muscles are attached to bones while smooth muscles are not. Skeletal muscle fibers are multi-nucleated while each smooth muscle cell has a single nucleus

SOLUTION: Molar mass of salt =64 Gain in mass when it forms dihydrate = 36 % gain = Find the equation of the tangent and normal to the curve y = x2 +1 at the point on the curve where x=1.

subject: Physics
questionText: Use the first law of thermodynamics to explain why the shaft of a bicycle pump heats up when the pump is used to inflate a tire. What other effect could lead to the heating?
correctAnswer: According to the first law of thermodynamics, when work is done on a gas, the internal energy increases and heat energy is generated. Work is done on the pump when the piston compresses the air and forces it into the tire. (On the 'upstroke', fresh air is drawn into the shaft before also being compressed- this leads to a continued warming of the shaft). Another effect that leads to heating is friction between the parts of the pump. Explain why the surface of a car tire has grooves on it

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Explain why endothermic reactions are supposed not to occur spontaneously.
correctAnswer: The products formed (thermodynamically) are less stable than the reactants. OR The internal energy of products is higher than the internal energy of the reactants

questionText: The combustion of methane to form CO2 and H2O is an exothermic reaction and yet it does not occur spontaneously. Why?
correctAnswer: The reaction needs external energy to overcome the activation energy. OR Energy is required to break the strong O-O bond oxygen before oxygen can react

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Explain why exothermic compounds are supposed to be stable compounds.
correctAnswer: When they are formed from their elements in their standard states they are more stable than the reactants. OR They contain less internal energy than the internal energy of the reactants put together

questionText: In certain horticultural plant species, red flower (R) is dominant over yellow flower (r) and tallness (T) is dominant over shortness (t). A plant grower in his attempt to produce offsprings that are heterozygous for both traits crossed a plant that is RrTT with a plant that is rrTt. Determine whether he made a good decision. (B)
correctAnswer: RrTT X rrTt RrTT gametes RT, rT rrTt gametes rT,rt For the plant to be heterozygous for both traits, the genotype should be RrTt rrTt RrTT rT rt RT RrTT RrTt rT rrTT rrTt From above, the chance of producing off-springs heterozygous for both traits is 25% (1/4). Not a good decision

questionText: a. Briefly explain how the light emitted by a hot body can be used to estimate temperature of the body. State the assumptions you make. b. The wavelength of the peak of the emission spectrum from a high temperature furnace was determined to be 3.05 µm. The wavelength for the peak in the solar radiation spectrum measured just outside the terrestrial atmosphere is 451 nm. If the temperature of the sun is approximately 6425 K and both the sun and the furnace can be considered as black bodies, estimate the temperature of the furnace. c. A second furnace is to be operated at twice the temperature of the furnace above. Find the wavelength expected for peak emission from the second furnace and find the ratio of the total radiant emission from the higher temperature furnace to that of the lower temperature furnace. (P)
correctAnswer: If a hot body is assumed to be a black body, the spectrum of light it emits depends on temperature. In particular, the wavelength of peak intensity in the spectrum satisfies the displacement law λmT = constant. Thus by determining the peak wavelength, the temperature of the body can be determined. Alternatively, the intensity at several wavelengths can be found and the black body radiation formula can then be used to find T

Explain why there are more flowers than fruits. (B)
correctAnswer: In order for fruits to form, a flower should be pollinated. Pollination requires a vector or carrier to transfer the pollen from the anther to the stigma. Hence in the absence of the vector or a carrier, pollination will not occur and that affects fruit formation. The flowers need to mature and produce pollen and stigma, hence all the immature flowers that are yet to be pollinated add up to the total number of flowers allowing them to outnumber the fruits. Pollination may not take place for fruits to develop if male and female flowers are produced at different times. The male pollen may not land on the stigma for fruits to develop if they are separated by significant distance

subject: Chemistry
questionText: A chromatographic experiment is to be performed to separate a solid mixture of two differently coloured compounds. The solids are only slightly soluble in petroleum ether but very soluble in chloroform. A column has been prepared for you. This is a glass tube containing the adsorbent in petroleum ether. Describe exactly how you would proceed to perform the experiment and separate the mixture. (C)
correctAnswer: 1. Weigh the desired amount of the solid mixture. 2. Dissolve it in the minimum volume of chloroform. 3. Drain the column until the solvent level drops to the level of the adsorbent. 4. Using a Pasteur pipette, transfer the concentrated chloroform solution to the top of the column. 5. Let the solution drain to the level of the adsorbent. 6. Add more petroleum ether and collect the eluent at the rate of about a drop per second. 7. When the two colours are separated, increase the polarity of the solvent/eluent. 8. Collect 5 or 10 cm3 fractions. 9. Combine fractions of the same colour. 10. Distil off most of the solvents and leave concentrated solutions in the fridge to crystallize or distil off all the solvents, combine residues of the same colour and recrystallize residues in suitable solvents

subject: Physics
questionText: Why is it that a bird perching on a high tension power line is not electrocuted? (P)
correctAnswer: There is too little potential difference across the bird for significant current flow. Because a power line is a good conductor, only a small potential difference is needed across it for ordinary current levels, so most of the potential drop occurs across the load when a current is carried. This means the potential drop across a small distance as that between the feet of a bird is rather small

subject: Physics

correctAnswer: 120 Hz; the light is produced by thermal radiation by the resistively heated filament and is independent of the polarity of the voltage so both positive and negative cycles of voltage give emission; the emission minimum is at the zero crossings

subject: Biology

correctAnswer: Fainting is a brief loss of consciousness caused by a temporary reduction in blood flow to the brain. When a person faints that person falls to a horizontal position and this improves the blood flow to the brain. Holding such a person in an upright position decreases blood flow to the head since blood has to be pumped against gravity. This could lead to death

subject: Physics
questionText: for both questions: A balloon is filled with helium gas and attached to a string. In that case, will the balloon remain at the same height, or will it fall to the ground as the air inside the glass container is pumped out? Explain
correctAnswer: The balloon will fall to the ground. Initially, the forces acting on the balloon are its weight and the upthrust. As the air is pumped out of the container, the density of the air decreases, and the upthrust reduces. Therefore, the weight of the balloon makes it fall to the ground

subject: Physics
questionText: Objects that are closer to your eyes appear larger than objects that are far away. For example, a pencil held at arms length appears larger than a storey building hundreds of meters away. Explain why this is so.
correctAnswer: The length (or size) of an image that is formed by the eye is proportional to the 'visual angle' at the eye - i.e. the angle subtended at the eye by the object. The larger the visual angle, the greater the apparent size of the object. Since the visual angle is largest when the object is close to the eye, such objects appear larger

subject: Maths
questionText: State the difference between an internal combustion engine and an external combustion engine and give an example of each, as applied to vehicular transportation.
correctAnswer: In an internal combustion engine, the working fluid is in direct contact with the combustible material; i.e. the combustible material is burnt inside the engine. In an external combustion engine, the working fluid is not in direct contact although it is in thermal contact; i.e. the combustible material is burnt outside the engine. Examples: Internal combustion engine: car engines; petrol, diesel engines, diesel- electric trains, motorbikes, etc; (corn mill engine). External combustion engine: steam locomotives, steam ships Find an equation of variation in which y varies as x and inversely as z2, y = 2, when x = 10 and z = 5

subject: Biology
questionText: When the leaves of the water lily or Nymphaea are pushed below the surface of water and released, they quickly float again and the water rolls off the surface of the leaves. What accounts for the rolling of water off the leaf surface and what is its significance?
correctAnswer: Water rolls off the leaf surface because the leaf surface is covered with a waxy cuticle. This prevents water from blocking the stomata and reducing the diffusion of CO2 into the leaf for photosynthesis One of the major problems faced by submerged aquatic plants is the low rate of diffusion of O2 and CO2. Give me two features of the ceratophylum that enhance the diffusion of O2 and CO2

questionText: How much pure water must be added to 10 liters of 4% salt solution in order to produce a 3%salt solution?
correctAnswer: 10/3 LITRES 3.33l, 3 1/3l SOLUTION: Let x liters of water be added to the 4% salt solution? subject: Biology
questionText: Sulphur dioxide SO2 is a poisonous gas produced when sulphur - containing fuels are burned. Give me three harmful effects of sulphur dioxide (SO2) pollution.
correctAnswer: It kills plant cells and thus reduces the rate of photosynthesis It dissolves in water vapor to produce acid rain which speeds up corrosion of metals and damages walls of buildings. It increases the acidity of water bodies and soils and this results in the death of organisms like fish In humans, it causes lung disease e.g. bronchitis. Lead pollution comes mainly from motor vehicle exhaust gases. Give me, three harmful effects of lead poisoning in children

subject: Physics
questionText: Explain why the bulb of a clinical thermometer is always small.
correctAnswer: This is to minimize the amount of heat energy taken by the thermometer itself during the measurement

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Two cars leave town A at the same time with one car traveling 15 km/hr faster than the other car. The faster car arrives at town B 3hrs later, ½ hour before the other car. Find the average speed of each car.
correctAnswer: 90 km / hr and 105 km/ hr SOLUTION: Let speed of slower car be v km/hr Distance = Speed × Time (V+15)3 = V(3½), hence 45 = ½ v and v = 90

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Two towns A and B are 320 km apart. A car leaves A for B and at the same time a second car leaves B for A. One car's speed is 20 km/hr more than the other. The two cars meet after two hours. Find the average speeds of the two cars.
correctAnswer: 70 km / hr and 90 km/hr SOLUTION: Let speed of slower car be v km/hr Distance = Speed × Time 2v + (v + 20)2 = 320, hence 4v + 40 = 320, and v = 70

subject: Biology
questionText: Will guttation in a humid area occur more in plants with plenty of stomata or in plants with few stomata? Explain your
correctAnswer: It will occur more in plants with few stomata because if stomata are few, less water will be lost by transpiration and the rest will have to be lost by guttation. Which generally decays faster, plants or animal tissue and why?

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Why do benzene or other unsaturated hydrocarbons burn with sooty flame?
correctAnswer: The carbon content per molecule is relatively high. Eg. Benzene C : H = 6: 6 or 1:1 Hexane C : H = 6: 14 or 3:7

subject: Physics
questionText: Benzene nucleus usually reacts with nucleophiles with difficulty but when it contains a type of substituent substitution becomes easier. What type of substitution would make nucleophilic substitution easier?
correctAnswer: Electron withdrawing groups. They reduce the charge density in the ring/on the carbons

subject: Physics
questionText: Explain why a nail is made with a sharp tip.
correctAnswer: A sharp tip means that less force is required from the hammer to drive the nail into a piece of wood, since the force per unit area at the tip will be greater for a sharper tip. The shape makes it easier to penetrate the wood

subject: Physics
questionText: Explain why it is easier to keep a wooden crate in motion over a rough floor, than it is to start moving it.
correctAnswer: This is because the co-efficient of static friction is higher than the co-efficient of kinetic friction, which means that the force of friction is greater for the stationary crate and therefore a larger force is required to get it to start moving it than is required to keep it in motion

subject: Chemistry
questionText: A fair coin is tossed three times. Find the probability that exactly two heads appear.
correctAnswer: 3/8 SOLUTION: A = {HHT, HTH, THH} Exactly two heads has three equally likely outcomes The samples space has = 8 equally outcomes Hence P(A) = 3/8

subject: Chemistry
questionText: A biased coin has a probability of a head being 1/3. It is tossed three times. Find the probability of having two heads.

subject: Maths
questionText: It is difficult to make out colours in dim light why?
correctAnswer: This is because the cones which are responsible for colour vision function best in bright light

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Prolonged strenuous exercise results in a lowering of blood pH. Why?
correctAnswer: Prolonged strenuous exercise results in the production of lactic acid and a lot of carbon dioxide. The latter dissolves in the water of plasma to form carbonic acid. This together with lactic acid, which is produced anaerobically, causes the lowering of the pH of blood

questionText: SO2 and NH3 are substances that are used as refrigerants. What do the substances have in common?
correctAnswer: They are both gaseous at room temperature and can easily be compressed to give liquids

questionText: If the refrigerant of your refrigerator is SO2 and there is a leakage, how could you tell that there is a leakage?
correctAnswer: SO2 is a pungent smelling gas

subject: Physics
questionText: The sum emits a great number of charged particles which stream out into space. Why are most of these particles unable to reach the surface of the Earth?
correctAnswer: The charged particles are trapped by the earth's magnetic field

questionText: Is the colour of the sun related to its surface temperature? (Explain your Answer)
correctAnswer: Yes, the colour of the sun is related to the surface temperature by the Stefan - Boltzmann law. The most intense wavelength of electromagnetic radiation emitted from every incandescent body depends on its temperature

subject: Biology
questionText: In using a pitfall trap to sample the population of small animals, a glass jar is often sunk into the pit. Explain why.
correctAnswer: The sides of a glass jar are smooth and slippery and so animals trapped cannot escape

subject: Biology
questionText: Explain why the capture - mark- release recapture method of determining the population of a species of animals in an area work best with animals whose movements are limited by geographic barriers , e.g. animals in a pond.
correctAnswer: This is because animals that have been captured, marked and released should have the same chance as animals unmarked animals. If the movements of animals are not limited, the random mixing of marked and unmarked animals will not be possible. Name one functional group in paracetamol

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Name one functional group in aspirin.
correctAnswer: Carboxylic acid / Alkanoic acid Estar / Alkanoate. Benzene ring / aromatic nucleus

subject: Physics
questionText: Describe the process of the transfer of heat energy in a metal by conduction.
correctAnswer: Conduction in a metal is due to the vibrations of atoms about their mean positions. Heat energy is also distributed through the metal by motion of electrons. Describe the process of the transfer of heat energy by convection

correctAnswer: 15 hours SOLUTION: time (hrs) rate Painter 10 1/10 Painter +assistant 6 1/6 1/10 +1/T = 1/6 Assistant T 1/T 1/T = 1/6-1/10 = (10-6)/60, T =15

questionText: Give three economic benefits of bees.
correctAnswer: Bees produce Honey which is used as food and sweetener Bee wax is used for making candles, floor waxes and polishes Bees are agents of pollination

subject: Biology
questionText: One economic importance of lichens is that they are used as indicator species. What does this mean?
correctAnswer: This means that they are used to estimate the extent of pollution in an area. This is because they will grow only where the pollution is below a certain level. Name three metals that react with water at room temperature

correctAnswer: It forms salts with more covalent character (due to its small atomic /ionic size)

subject: Physics
questionText: You are in a car that is moving at a constant speed of 75km/h. A car behind you sounds its horn. This other car is also moving at 75km/h in the same direction as your car. How will the frequency which you hear compare with the actual frequency of the horn? Explain your
correctAnswer: The frequency heard will be the same as the frequency of the horn. This is because both cars are moving in the same direction with the same velocity, and therefore the relative velocity of one car to the other is zero. questionText: Which has a higher percentage of water vapour, inhaled or exhaled air and why?
correctAnswer: Exhaled air has a higher percentage of water vapour because the respiratory organs have moist surfaces and add water to inhaled air

questionText: Which has a higher percentage of nitrogen inhaled or exhaled air and why?
correctAnswer: There is no difference in the percentage of nitrogen in inhaled and exhaled air. This is because nitrogen in inhaled air is not used for gaseous exchange and therefore its percentage remains the same

subject: Physics
questionText: Explain how Bohr's model of the atom explained the absorption and emission of radiation.
correctAnswer: According to Bohr's atomic model, the electrons are in orbits around the nucleus of the atom, and these orbits define the energy levels of the atom. Absorption of radiation occurs when an electron receives just enough energy from incident photons to jump from a lower energy level to a higher energy level. Emission occurs when the electron jumps from a higher energy level to a lower energy level

subject: Maths
questionText: In Rutherford's scattering experiment, what would have been observed if a low- density beam of neutrons were used instead of alpha particles?
correctAnswer: Since neutrons are uncharged, they are most likely to pass right through the metallic foil. Any deviations caused will be due to mechanical collisions and not electrostatic repulsion, so the likelihood of a deflection through a wide angle is small

correctAnswer: Carbon dating or synthesis of organic molecules containing C14 for tracer studies

questionText: How are carbon-14 atoms produced.
correctAnswer: By bombardment of nitrogen nuclei by neutrons

subject: Biology
questionText: Which of these 2 plants, venus flytrap or Canna lily would you expect to do well in a nitrogen deficient soil and why?
correctAnswer: Venus fly trap is an insectivorous plant and can therefore obtain its supply of nitrates by digesting small animals. It will therefore do better in a nitrogen deficient soil than canna lily which is just a rhizome

subject: Biology
questionText: Which of these 2 plants, groundnuts or cocoyam, would you expect to do well in a nitrogen-deficient soil and why?
correctAnswer: Groundnut is a leguminous plant and has nodules on its roots which contain bacteria that can fix atmospheric nitrogen and make it available to the plant. Cocoyam is a corm and cannot obtain nitrates from any other source

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Distinguish between cold-cathode emission and thermionic emission from a metallic surface
correctAnswer: Cold-cathode emission occurs when electrons are ejected from the surface of a metal by the action of an electric field alone; whereas thermionic emission occurs when electrons are emitted from the surface due to the high temperature of the metal

questionText: Mention the three ways in which a bipolar junction transistor is connected in a circuit.
correctAnswer: Common base; Common emitter; and Common collector

questionText: Organisms in the phylum Oomycota e.g. slime moulds, are often referred to as fungus-like protists. What differentiates slime moulds from true fungi?
correctAnswer: Slime moulds produce spores that have flagella and are thus mobile while the spores of true fungi have no flagella and are non-mobile. correctAnswer: The pulse is a series of dilations that spread along the arteries as a result of the pumping action of the heart. subject: Physics
questionText: Explain why an x-ray tube is evacuated and kept under vacuum.
correctAnswer: Since there are high electric fields and fast moving electrons inside the tube when in operation, any atom within the tube will be ionized by collision with the electrons. When this happens, the positive ions created will be accelerated towards the cathode, which they strike at high velocity. Continuous bombardment of the cathode by the relatively heavy ions will result in damage to the cathode and a shorter lifetime of the tube

subject: Biology
questionText: If someone is bitten by a snake, would you inject the person with whole blood containing the anti-venom or serum containing the anti-venom? Explain the reason for your choice.
correctAnswer: It would be preferable to inject the person with serum containing the anti-venom. This is because if whole blood is injected, it would include the red blood cells and if the victim of the snake bite is in a different blood group, his or her red blood cells will clump together (agglutinate) and cause the person's death. With serum, the blood group of the recipient does not matter

questionText: If someone is bitten by a snake, how can you determine whether the snake is poisonous or not by an examination of the bite marks?
correctAnswer: If the snake is not poisonous, it will leave marks of 2 rows of teeth but no fang marks, but if the snake is poisonous, it will leave two deep marks of the 2 fangs. PREAMBLE TO BOTH SCHOOLS: Express as partial fractions

subject: Physics
questionText: Explain why a semiconductor with a large forbidden gap and an empty conduction band can be a good dielectric material.
correctAnswer: A dielectric material is an insulator. With a large forbidden gap, a lot of energy is needed to move an electron from the valence band to the conduction band. If the valence band is empty, then such a semiconductor will be an insulator until sufficient energy is applied to cause electrons to move into the conduction band

subject: Physics
questionText: When an intrinsic semiconductor is exposed to elevated temperatures, it becomes more capable of sustaining the flow of electric current .This is especially so for semiconductors with a small band gap. Explain how this happens.
correctAnswer: For a small band gap, it does not require much energy to make an electron move from the valence to the conduction band. So, the electron can absorb the thermal energy and thereby cross the forbidden gap. Once the electrons are in the conduction band they are able to conduct electricity. questionText: The average of 10 students is 22 years. If the age of a teacher is included the average age is increased to 25 years. Find the age of the teacher.
correctAnswer: 55years SOLUTION: let age of teacher be x years 220 + x = 25(11) = 275, hence x = 275 - 220 = 55

correctAnswer: 50years SOLUTION: Let age of teacher be x years 190 + x = 20(12) = 240, hence x = 240 - 190 = 50

correctAnswer: Pioneer community What name is given to the final stable community which is most productive and can be sustained by the environment?

correctAnswer: Pancreas Name an organ that acts as both a excretory organ and a sense organ

subject: Physics
questionText: In the measurement of blood pressure, two numbers are recorded, e.g. 120/80. Explain what these numbers stand for.
correctAnswer: 120 represents the systolic pressure i.e. the pressure in the arteries when the heart contracts while the 80 represents the diastolic pressure i.e. the pressure in the arteries when the heart relaxes.. Which of the chambers of the heart has the thickest muscular wall and why?

subject: Chemistry
questionText: Explain why although the formula of ethanoic acid is C2H4O2 , and that of carbonic is H2CO3 , the former is a monobasic acid while the later is a dibasic acid.
correctAnswer: Ethanoic acid can donate only one proton to a base whilst carbonic acid can donate 2. Why is AlCl3 used as an acid sometimes?

subject: Physics
questionText: It is possible to determine the distance of the moon from the earth by reflecting a beam of radio waves from the moon's surface and measuring the time it takes for the reflected signal to be received. Why it is not possible to determine the earth-sun distance in this manner?
correctAnswer: It is not possible because the sun behaves as a black-body, i.e. it is a perfect emitter as well as absorber of radiation. Therefore, the sun will absorb the radio waves and no reflection will be observed

subject: Chemistry
questionText: What do you think is the main disadvantage of wilting?
correctAnswer: The folding of leaves reduces the surface area of the leaves exposed to direct sunlight and this reduces photosynthesis. In Chemistry what are standard solutions?

correctAnswer: SOLUTION: b = 3i - 2j is perpendicular to a = 2i + 3j The corresponding unit vector is 14. a = 3i - 4j

subject: Physics
questionText: Under what condition is the centre of gravity of an object equal to the center of mass?
correctAnswer: The centre of gravity is equal to the centre of mass when the gravitational field is uniform and the acceleration due to gravity is the same for all parts of the object

subject: Physics
questionText: The star, Alpha Centauri is 4.4 light years, 4.4 x 1013 kilometers from the earth. Is there any gravitational attraction between the planet earth and this galaxy? Explain your
correctAnswer: Yes. From Newton's theory of gravity, any object with mass will experience a Force due to the mass of all other objects. Even though the distance is so large, The force of gravity will still act though the force between the earth and Alpha Centauri will be very small on account of the great distance

correctAnswer: The fovea of the retina is specialized for colour vision. This is because most of the cones which are responsible for colour vision are concentrated here

subject: Biology
questionText: If a seed imbibes water, its weight increases. Would you regard this as growth? Explain.
correctAnswer: No, this is not growth. This is because growth is an incresase in the DRY weight of an organism through cell division and cell enlargement

subject: Biology
questionText: Is Guttation likely to occur in forest trees or savanna trees? Explain your answer
correctAnswer: It is likely to occur in forest trees. Guttation is the exudation from plants of water in the liquid form. It is therefore likely to occur in warm HUMID areas where forest trees are found. The savanna areas are dry and gultation is not likely to occur here

questionText: How would you obtain dry ammonia gas?
correctAnswer: By passing it through a tube packed with calcium oxide

subject: Physics
questionText: A sailing ship or a boat equipped with sails uses the kinetic energy of the wind to move over the sea, i.e., the wind exerts a force on the sails and this moves the ship forward. Does this mean that the ship will always move in the same direction in which the wind is blowing? Explain you
correctAnswer: No. By adjusting the position of the sail and the rudder, the resultant force on the ship can be made to act in a desired direction, even if the wind is not blowing in that particular direction

subject: Physics
questionText: You are in a bus that is traveling at 70km/h on a highway when a truck traveling at 90km/h overtakes your bus on the left hand side. A force will be exerted on your bus due to the pressure difference that is created. In what direction will this force act? Forward (b) to the left (c) to the right (d) backward? Explain your
correctAnswer: (b).The force will act towards the left; i.e., from right to left. This is because the wind speed between the truck and the bus will be increased as the truck passes by, and therefore the pressure will be lower on the left side and higher on the right side of the bus. This difference in pressure will result in a force acting towards the left. The pituitary gland is often referred to as the master gland. Why?

questionText: In cold climates, rooms are often warmed by radiators or electric heaters. Where is it more efficient to mount a heater in the room: closer to the floor or closer to the ceiling? Explain your
correctAnswer: It is more efficient to mount the heater closer to the floor. This is because warm air will rise by convection, causing the heat to be distributed throughout the room

subject: Physics
questionText: Distinguish between temperature and heat.
correctAnswer: Temperature is a measure of the degree of hotness or coldness of a body, whereas heat is the energy that is transferred between two objects/ reservoirs that are at different temperatures

subject: Physics

correctAnswer: The two objects are at the same temperature and any heat energy transferred between them will not change the temperature of either of them

correctAnswer: This is because energy is lost at each trophic level of a food chain

correctAnswer: Primary producers i.e. plants do not have all the essential amino acids that humans need (also plants have cellulose which is not digested)

questionText: The emissions from a radioactive substance are monitored in a laboratory with a Geiger-Muller counter. The radioactive substance has a half-life of several years. correctAnswer: The number of counts will decrease. This is because the GM tube measures the activity of the sample, which is a function of time and decays exponentially

questionText: The activity of two samples of a radioactive material is monitored in a laboratory. One of the samples has a mass of 5g and the other has a mass of 15g. Is the decay constant the same for both samples? Explain your
correctAnswer: The decay constant is the same for both samples, because it is independent of the mass of the sample

subject: Biology
questionText: In animals, the term incompatibility is used to describe the interaction between the tissues of two organisms that results in the rejection of a graft. What does the term mean when applied to higher plants?
correctAnswer: The term when applied to higher plants refers to the mechanism that ensures that pollen from a plant does not grow on the stigma of the same plant. (This prevents self fertilization)

subject: Biology
questionText: Fertilization in flowering plants is often described as double. Why?
correctAnswer: This is because in flowering plants, there is fertilization (i.e. fusion of male nucleus) with both the egg cell and the polar nuclei

correctAnswer: Lack of vitamin A causes night blindness.
